2841:// TestExecuteBeadPromptIsXMLTagged verifies that the synthesized execute-bead
2845:func TestExecuteBeadPromptIsXMLTagged(t *testing.T) {

2841	// TestExecuteBeadPromptIsXMLTagged verifies that the synthesized execute-bead
2842	// prompt is emitted as a well-structured XML document with the tags required
2843	// by FEAT-006's Prompt Rationalizer Contract. It also guards against regression
2844	// to the old markdown-heading-only prompt structure.
2845	func TestExecuteBeadPromptIsXMLTagged(t *testing.T) {
2846		workDir := t.TempDir()
2847		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
2848			ID:          "xml-bead",
2849			Title:       "Adopt XML-tagged execute-bead prompt template",
2850			Status:      bead.StatusOpen,
2851			Priority:    0,
2852			IssueType:   bead.DefaultType,
2853			Parent:      "ddx-parent",
2854			Description: "Replace the markdown-heading prompt with an XML-tagged structure so downstream tooling can diff and validate sections deterministically.",
2855			Acceptance:  "Prompt is XML-tagged with <execute-bead>, <bead>, <governing>, and <instructions>.",
2856			Labels:      []string{"area:agent", "area:docs"},
2857			Extra:       map[string]any{"spec-id": "FEAT-XML-TEST"},
2858		})
2859		specPath := filepath.Join(workDir, "docs", "feat-xml.md")
2860		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(specPath), 0o755))
2861		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(specPath, []byte(`---
2862	ddx:
2863	  id: FEAT-XML-TEST
2864	  title: XML Test Spec
2865	---
2866	# XML Test Spec
2867	`), 0o644))
2868	
2869		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
2870			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111cafe",
2871			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222beef",
2872		}
2873		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "mock"}}
2874		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
2875		seedDefaultExecuteBeads(t, workDir)
2876		f.AgentRunnerOverride = runner
2877		f.executeBeadGitOverride = git
2878		f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride = git
2879		f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride = fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit(git)
2880	
2881		_ = runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "xml-bead")
2882	
2883		require.NotEmpty(t, runner.last.PromptFile)
2884		promptRaw, err := os.ReadFile(runner.last.PromptFile)
2885		require.NoError(t, err)
2886		promptText := string(promptRaw)
2887	
2888		// Required root and subsection tags.
2889		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<execute-bead>")
2890		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "</execute-bead>")
2891		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `<bead id="xml-bead">`)
2892		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "</bead>")
2893		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<title>Adopt XML-tagged execute-bead prompt template</title>")
2894		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<description>")
2895		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "</description>")
2896		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<acceptance>")
2897		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "</acceptance>")
2898		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<labels>area:agent, area:docs</labels>")
2899		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `parent="ddx-parent"`)
2900		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `spec-id="FEAT-XML-TEST"`)
2901		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `base-rev="aaaa1111cafe"`)
2902		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `<metadata `)
2903		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<governing>")
2904		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "</governing>")
2905		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `<ref id="FEAT-XML-TEST"`)
2906		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<instructions>")
2907		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "</instructions>")
2908	
2909		// Regression guard: no markdown-heading-only sections.
2910		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "# Execute Bead\n")
2911		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "## Bead\n")
2912		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "## Description\n")
2913		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "## Acceptance Criteria\n")
2914		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "## Governing References\n")
2915		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "## Execution Rules\n")
2916	
2917		// The prompt must be parseable as a well-formed XML document.
2918		decoder := xml.NewDecoder(bytes.NewBufferString(promptText))
2919		for {
2920			_, tokErr := decoder.Token()
2921			if tokErr == io.EOF {
2922				break
2923			}
2924			require.NoError(t, tokErr, "prompt must be well-formed XML: %s", promptText)
2925		}
2926	}

commit 088c2e7c31ab3ffcbe8953009967c836e7c11a92
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Fri Apr 24 02:06:16 2026 -0400

    test(cmd): migrate TestExecuteBeadEmbeddedAgentStateRedirected to real git + script harness [ddx-c55119a7]
    
    Replaces fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner with an isolated real-git
    repo and the script harness driven by a directive file. The directive
    brackets a simulated embedded-agent write to $DDX_SESSION_LOG_DIR with
    ls -A snapshots of the worker worktree root, so the end-to-end wiring
    (ExecuteBead -> RunOptions.SessionLogDir -> runner) is exercised through
    real git and real Land() rather than a canned Result + same-rev pair.
    
    The script harness now interpolates $DDX_SESSION_LOG_DIR from
    opts.SessionLogDir; without it, directives cannot faithfully simulate a
    harness that writes session/telemetry state to the override dir.
    
    Parent: ddx-d9df348d. concerns.md §testing: no mocks, period.

 cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go | 166 ++++++++++++++++++++++---------------
 cli/internal/agent/script.go       |   7 +-
 2 files changed, 103 insertions(+), 70 deletions(-)

commit 088c2e7c31ab3ffcbe8953009967c836e7c11a92
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Fri Apr 24 02:06:16 2026 -0400

    test(cmd): migrate TestExecuteBeadEmbeddedAgentStateRedirected to real git + script harness [ddx-c55119a7]
    
    Replaces fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner with an isolated real-git
    repo and the script harness driven by a directive file. The directive
    brackets a simulated embedded-agent write to $DDX_SESSION_LOG_DIR with
    ls -A snapshots of the worker worktree root, so the end-to-end wiring
    (ExecuteBead -> RunOptions.SessionLogDir -> runner) is exercised through
    real git and real Land() rather than a canned Result + same-rev pair.
    
    The script harness now interpolates $DDX_SESSION_LOG_DIR from
    opts.SessionLogDir; without it, directives cannot faithfully simulate a
    harness that writes session/telemetry state to the override dir.
    
    Parent: ddx-d9df348d. concerns.md §testing: no mocks, period.

1069	// TestExecuteBeadResolvesPathStyleSpecID verifies that a bead whose spec-id is
1070	// a relative path (e.g. "workflows/README.md") — not an ID from the docgraph —
1071	// is resolved into a governing reference whose ID and Path both equal the given
1072	// path, and that the synthesized prompt surfaces that path. Exercises real git
1073	// + RealLandingGitOps via the script harness — no fakes for the components
1074	// under test. The script harness is driven by a no-op directive; the
1075	// orchestrator synthesizes the prompt from the seeded bead.
1076	func TestExecuteBeadResolvesPathStyleSpecID(t *testing.T) {
1077		workDir := t.TempDir()

1080			parent := os.Environ()

1084					continue
1085				}
1086				env = append(env, kv)
1087			}
1088			return env
1089		}
1090		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
1091			t.Helper()
1092			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
1093			cmd.Dir = workDir
1094			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
1095			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
1096			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
1097			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
1098		}
1099	
1100		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
1101		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
1102		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
1103		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
1104		runGit("add", ".")
1105		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
1106	
1107		// Path-style spec target: a file at workflows/README.md with NO docgraph
1108		// id frontmatter, so ResolveGoverningRefs must fall through to the path
1109		// resolver rather than matching against docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig.
1110		specPath := filepath.Join(workDir, "workflows", "README.md")
1111		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(specPath), 0o755))
1112		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(specPath, []byte("# Workflow\n"), 0o644))
1113		runGit("add", "workflows/README.md")
1114		runGit("commit", "-m", "docs: add workflow readme")
1115	
1116		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
1117			ID:          "path-bead",
1118			Title:       "Resolve path style spec ids",
1119			Status:      bead.StatusOpen,
1120			Priority:    0,
1121			IssueType:   bead.DefaultType,
1122			Description: "Make sure path-style spec-ids resolve to the on-disk file.",
1123			Acceptance:  "Governing reference carries the relative path unchanged.",
1124			Extra:       map[string]any{"spec-id": "workflows/README.md"},
1125		})
1126		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
1127		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
1128		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
1129	
1130		// Script harness directive: no-op. The assertion target is the synthesized
1131		// prompt file, not any worker output.
1132		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
1133		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte("no-op\n"), 0o644))
1134	
1135		// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
1136		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
1137		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
1138	
1139		root := f.NewRootCommand()
1140		out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "path-bead", "--json",
1141			"--from", baseSHA,
1142			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
1143		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
1144		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
1145	
1146		require.NotEmpty(t, res.PromptFile)
1147		promptPath := filepath.Join(workDir, filepath.FromSlash(res.PromptFile))
1148		require.FileExists(t, promptPath)
1149		promptRaw, err := os.ReadFile(promptPath)
1150		require.NoError(t, err)
1151		promptText := string(promptRaw)
1152		// The path-style spec-id must appear unchanged in the synthesized prompt
1153		// (both as ID and Path — ResolveGoverningRefs sets them equal for path
1154		// resolution). The bead title and description round out the synthesis
1155		// assertions so a regression in prompt building is also caught here.
1156		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "workflows/README.md")
1157		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "Resolve path style spec ids")
1158		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "Make sure path-style spec-ids resolve to the on-disk file.")
1159		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "Governing reference carries the relative path unchanged.")

240	// runExecuteBead invokes the execute-bead command through the cobra tree and returns
241	// the parsed JSON result. It extracts the JSON object from the combined output,
242	// skipping any leading note/warning lines written to stderr.
243	func runExecuteBead(t *testing.T, f *CommandFactory, git *fakeExecuteBeadGit, beadID string, extraArgs ...string) agent.ExecuteBeadResult {
244		t.Helper()
245		root := f.NewRootCommand()
246		args := append([]string{"agent", "execute-bead", beadID, "--json"}, extraArgs...)
247		out, err := executeCommand(root, args...)
248		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
249		return parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
250	}
251	
252	func parseExecuteBeadJSON(t *testing.T, out string) agent.ExecuteBeadResult {
253		t.Helper()
254		// Strip any non-JSON prefix lines (e.g. stderr notes written to the shared buffer).
255		jsonStart := strings.Index(out, "{")
256		require.NotEqual(t, -1, jsonStart, "output should contain JSON: %s", out)
257		jsonPart := out[jsonStart:]
258		var res agent.ExecuteBeadResult
259		dec := json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewBufferString(jsonPart))
260		require.NoError(t, dec.Decode(&res), "output should be valid JSON: %s", jsonPart)
261		return res
262	}
263

func TestExecuteBeadPromptIsXMLTagged(t *testing.T) {
	workDir := t.TempDir()
	seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
		ID:          "xml-bead",
		Title:       "Adopt XML-tagged execute-bead prompt template",
		Status:      bead.StatusOpen,
		Priority:    0,
		IssueType:   bead.DefaultType,
		Parent:      "ddx-parent",
		Description: "Replace the markdown-heading prompt with an XML-tagged structure so downstream tooling can diff and validate sections deterministically.",
		Acceptance:  "Prompt is XML-tagged with <execute-bead>, <bead>, <governing>, and <instructions>.",
		Labels:      []string{"area:agent", "area:docs"},
		Extra:       map[string]any{"spec-id": "FEAT-XML-TEST"},
	})
	specPath := filepath.Join(workDir, "docs", "feat-xml.md")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(specPath), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(specPath, []byte(`---
ddx:
  id: FEAT-XML-TEST
  title: XML Test Spec
---
# XML Test Spec
`), 0o644))

	git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
		mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111cafe",
		wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222beef",
	}
	runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "mock"}}
	f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
	seedDefaultExecuteBeads(t, workDir)
	f.AgentRunnerOverride = runner
	f.executeBeadGitOverride = git
	f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride = git
	f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride = fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit(git)

	_ = runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "xml-bead")

	require.NotEmpty(t, runner.last.PromptFile)
	promptRaw, err := os.ReadFile(runner.last.PromptFile)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	promptText := string(promptRaw)
input.new_string:
func TestExecuteBeadPromptIsXMLTagged(t *testing.T) {
	workDir := t.TempDir()

	scrubEnv := func() []string {
		parent := os.Environ()
		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
		for _, kv := range parent {
			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
				continue
			}
			env = append(env, kv)
		}
		return env
	}
	runGit := func(args ...string) string {
		t.Helper()
		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
		cmd.Dir = workDir
		cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
		require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
	}

	runGit("init", "-b", "main")
	runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
	runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
	runGit("add", ".")
	runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")

	specPath := filepath.Join(workDir, "docs", "feat-xml.md")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(specPath), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(specPath, []byte(`---
ddx:
  id: FEAT-XML-TEST
  title: XML Test Spec
---
# XML Test Spec
`), 0o644))
	runGit("add", "docs/feat-xml.md")
	runGit("commit", "-m", "docs: add xml spec")

	seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
		ID:          "xml-bead",
		Title:       "Adopt XML-tagged execute-bead prompt template",
		Status:      bead.StatusOpen,
		Priority:    0,
		IssueType:   bead.DefaultType,
		Parent:      "ddx-parent",
		Description: "Replace the markdown-heading prompt with an XML-tagged structure so downstream tooling can diff and validate sections deterministically.",
		Acceptance:  "Prompt is XML-tagged with <execute-bead>, <bead>, <governing>, and <instructions>.",
		Labels:      []string{"area:agent", "area:docs"},
		Extra:       map[string]any{"spec-id": "FEAT-XML-TEST"},
	})
	runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
	runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
	baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")

	// Script harness directive: no-op. The assertion target is the synthesized
	// prompt file, not worker output.
	directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte("no-op\n"), 0o644))

	// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
	f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
	f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})

	res := runExecuteBead(t, f, nil, "xml-bead",
		"--from", baseSHA,
		"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)

	require.NotEmpty(t, res.PromptFile)
	promptPath := filepath.Join(workDir, filepath.FromSlash(res.PromptFile))
	promptRaw, err := os.ReadFile(promptPath)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	promptText := string(promptRaw)

repo and the script harness. The XML-tagged prompt assertions now run

mainHeadRev. All prior assertions preserved.
